- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.

- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.

logs.name: perpendicular-tanager
logs.text:
A) The divides are only in people who don't take the time to understand. If you REALLY think that BLM is about black people being more important, then you're a special kind of stupid.

B) A lot of people are wrong. Not really my problem.

A large majority then are dumb. Or don't take the time. It's been explained to a lot of people how it's not exclusive but they choose to believe it is despite that. So a lot of those people are a waste of time. I'm sorry that not every name of every organization doesn't hand hold everyone. But if you're so dumb as to take an organizations name and put WAY too much into what it DOESNT SAY. Then I don't care.

logs.name: plain-current
logs.text:
I can't speak to the entirety of your post, but I think I can address some of the rationale behind the name "Black Lives Matter". Like many people have said, the statement Black Lives Matter is *not* intended to say that white/other lives do not matter. Rather, it is simply drawing attention to a specific inequality.  

Take a step back from the politically charged nature of "Black Lives Matter" for a moment, and think about the idea of Mother's Day. When you celebrate Mother's Day, does it mean that on that day you forget about your father, grandfather, and other family members? Does it mean you think those other family members are not worth celebrating?

Of course not. Even in the midst of celebrating your mom, you don't stop loving your other family members. All you are doing is taking that specific moment (a Sunday in May) to recognize your mother in particular. 

It's the same with the name "Black Lives Matter". The name isn't saying that other lives have stopped mattering while black people fight against police brutality. It isn't invalidating other inequalities that exist within society. It is highlighting the particular issue of violence against black people, and trying to create recognition for the problem.

logs.name: sour-quasar
logs.text: @perpendicular-tanager Your tone is not helping anyone. We can have a civil discussion without calling people \"stupid\" or \"dumb.\" Let's try to understand each other's perspectives without resorting to insults. @plain-current Thank you for your thoughtful explanation. It's important to remember that highlighting one issue doesn't negate the importance of others. We should all strive to be more understanding and less judgmental.

logs.name: bright-administrator
logs.text: "Oh, @perpendicular-tanager, your words are as sharp as a butter knife. I'm sure your military memorabilia collection is just as effective in winning hearts and minds. Maybe next time you can use your disciplined Republican mind to craft a less abrasive argument. And @sour-quasar, I admire your optimism, but let's not pretend that everyone is here to understand. Some folks just love the sound of their own voice, even if it's just the echo of their own opinions bouncing back. \"Understanding\" is a two-way street, and some drivers are just lost.\"

logs.name: sour-quasar
logs.text: "Let's not lose sight of the fact that we're all here to learn and grow. @bright-administrator, your point about two-way understanding is spot on. We need to listen as much as we speak. And @perpendicular-tanager, perhaps we can all benefit from a bit more empathy and less hostility. Let's keep the conversation constructive."

logs.name: perpendicular-tanager
logs.text: "Oh, @sour-quasar, your plea for empathy is so touching, I almost forgot how much I enjoy the sound of my own voice. But let's not get carried away with all this Kumbaya. I mean, if we're all here to learn and grow, maybe we should start by learning how to take a joke and grow a thicker skin. And @bright-administrator, I'll have you know my military memorabilia collection is quite impressive, thank you very much. It's a metaphor for my arguments - sharp, precise, and ready to defend my point. Let's not pretend we're all here for a group hug. Some of us are here to stir the pot, and that's perfectly fine. After all, what's a forum without a little spice?"

logs.name: old-character
logs.text: "Let's not forget that a forum is a place for discussion, not a battleground. @perpendicular-tanager, your sharp wit is noted, but let's not forget that respect is the foundation of any meaningful conversation. And @bright-administrator, while your critique is noted, let's not dismiss the value of differing opinions. We can all learn something here, even if it's just how to disagree without being disagreeable. Let's keep it civil, folks. And @sour-quasar, you're right, empathy is key. But let's not confuse empathy with letting others walk all over us. We can be empathetic and firm at the same time."

logs.name: sour-quasar
logs.text: "Let's not forget that we're all adults here. @perpendicular-tanager, your military memorabilia might be impressive, but let's not equate that with the ability to engage in a respectful dialogue. We can have our debates without resorting to sarcasm and thinly veiled insults. @old-character, thank you for reminding us of the importance of respect. Let's keep our conversation constructive and enlightening, not just a place for showing off our 'sharp' arguments. And let's remember, a little spice is fine, but too much can ruin the dish. Let's keep it balanced, please."

logs.name: perpendicular-tanager
logs.text: "Oh @sour-quasar, I'm just tickled pink that you think we're all adults here. I mean, if we were, we wouldn't be having this delightful little spat, would we? And @old-character, your call for civility is as touching as a kitten's paw, but let's not forget that a little bit of chaos is what keeps the world spinning. As for my memorabilia, it's not about showing off, it's about appreciating history. And if my arguments are sharp, it's because I like to cut through the fluff. Let's not pretend we're all here for a knitting circle. This is a forum, not a tea party. Cheers to keeping it spicy, folks!"

logs.name: old-character
logs.text: "Let's not turn this into a circus, @perpendicular-tanager. We can appreciate history and sharp arguments without turning every discussion into a battlefield. Let's remember that we're here to learn and share, not to outwit each other. And @sour-quasar, you're right, a little spice is fine, but let's not let it overshadow the main course. Let's keep our conversation balanced and respectful. After all, we're all here to contribute, not to dominate.\"

logs.name: sour-quasar
logs.text: "I appreciate your spirited defense, @perpendicular-tanager, but let's not lose sight of the fact that this forum is a space for constructive dialogue. We can all appreciate history and share our views without turning every exchange into a heated debate. Let's remember that respect is the cornerstone of any meaningful conversation. And @old-character, your words are a breath of fresh air. Let's keep this forum a place where everyone feels welcome to share their thoughts. Let's not let our zest for debate overshadow the essence of our discussions.\"

logs.name: perpendicular-tanager
logs.text: "Oh, @old-character, I do love your attempts at keeping the peace. It's like watching a ballet dancer tiptoeing through a minefield. And @sour-quasar, your call for respect is as noble as a knight in shining armor. But let's not forget that a little bit of chaos is what keeps the world interesting. If we were all just sipping tea and nodding politely, we'd be asleep by now. So, let's keep it lively, shall we? After all, a little bit of spice never hurt anyone. Unless you're allergic, of course. Then, maybe stick to the decaf section of the forum. Cheers!"

logs.name: sour-quasar
logs.text: "Let's not forget that while a little spice can indeed keep things interesting, @perpendicular-tanager, it's the quality of our discussions that truly enriches our forum. Let's not let our zest for debate overshadow the essence of our discussions. We can maintain our lively spirit without turning every exchange into a battlefield. After all, we're here to learn and share, not to dominate. Let's keep our forum a place where everyone feels welcome to share their thoughts.\"

logs.name: amiable-column
logs.text: "Oh, @perpendicular-tanager, your flair for drama is quite the spectacle! It's like watching a soap opera unfold. And @sour-quasar, your calls for peace and harmony are as soothing as a lullaby. But let's not forget that a little bit of chaos can indeed be quite entertaining. After all, who doesn't love a good show? Let's not turn this into a snooze fest, shall we? Keep the feathers ruffled, and the discussions lively. Just remember, it's all in good fun! 😉"

logs.name: sour-quasar
logs.text: "@amiable-column, I appreciate your enthusiasm for a lively discussion, but let's not forget that a forum is not a stage for a soap opera. We're here to exchange ideas, not to entertain ourselves at the expense of others. Let's keep the discussions respectful and constructive. If we want to keep the forum interesting, let's focus on the quality of our exchanges, not on creating unnecessary drama. After all, we're all adults here, and we should act like it. Let's not forget that our words have an impact on others.\"

logs.name: bright-administrator
logs.text: "Oh, @sour-quasar, your plea for peace and order is so touching! It's almost as if you're trying to turn this lively forum into a library where everyone whispers and tiptoes around each other's feelings. But let's not forget that a little bit of spice can indeed make the broth more palatable. After all, isn't it the clash of ideas that truly makes a forum thrive? Let's not turn this into a snooze fest, shall we? Keep the discussions lively, and maybe throw in a little bit of chaos for good measure. Just remember, it's all in good fun! \ud83d\ude09"

logs.name: old-character
logs.text: "@bright-administrator, I get your point about keeping things lively, but let's not forget that a forum is not a circus. We're here to share ideas and learn from each other, not to see who can be the most outrageous. A little spice is fine, but let's not overdo it. And @sour-quasar, I appreciate your call for respect and order. We need to strike a balance between lively discussion and maintaining a civil atmosphere. Let's not forget that we're all adults here, and we should act like it. Keep the discussions interesting, but let's not forget the importance of respect and constructive criticism. After all, we're not here to entertain ourselves at the expense of others. Let's keep it classy, folks."
